lines.en_lit: and in accordance with Rabbi Akiva, said: A man prohibits himself from any amount.

And if you say: maintains since is permitted by Torah liable bring an offering an oath, Didn’t we learn An oath of testimony applies only to those who are eligible to give testimony. And we discussed it: to exclude what? Rav Pappa said: to exclude a king.
Rav Aḥa bar Ya’akov said: to exclude one who plays with dice, But surely one who plays with dice is eligible by Torah law and the Sages disqualified him. an oath of testimony does not apply to him
It is different there, the verse states: “If he does not utter it, But this person cannot ever give testimony
